exam_format: Computer-based, fixed-form (not adaptive); at Prometric test centers only — no at-home option. Four sections with optional scheduled breaks between each section. Total seated time approximately 7 hours 30 minutes (includes tutorial, void option, breaks). Section order is fixed: CPBS, CARS, BBFL, PSBF. Void option: candidate may void all scores at the end of the exam at no cost; voided scores are not reported.
exam_price_notes: $335 USD standard registration; $375 USD late registration. Fee assistance program (FAP) available for eligible applicants: reduces fee to $135. Rescheduling and cancellation fees apply; see aamc.org for current fee schedule.

testing_centers.notes: MCAT is available exclusively at Prometric test centers. No at-home or online proctored option is available. Register via the AAMC registration portal at students-residents.aamc.org. Seats are limited; register early, especially for spring testing dates (most competitive). Test centers are located throughout the United States, Canada, and select international locations.

id_requirements: One government-issued, unexpired photo ID required (e.g., driver's license, passport). Name on ID must exactly match name on AAMC registration. A second acceptable form of ID is recommended as backup. Biometric data (fingerprint or palm vein scan) collected at some Prometric centers.
exam_rules: No personal items permitted in the testing room. Scratch paper (laminated noteboard and marker) provided at the test center — no outside materials. No unauthorized electronic devices, notes, food, or drinks in the testing room (small snack/drink for break consumption only, stored in locker). Bathroom breaks permitted between sections only (breaks are scheduled). Candidates may opt to void all scores at the end of the exam; once voided, scores cannot be recovered. Attempting to access testing materials or communicate with others during the exam may result in score cancellation and a ban from future MCAT administrations.
renewal_options: MCAT scores are not certifications requiring renewal. Most medical schools accept MCAT scores up to 3 years old at the time of matriculation; some schools accept scores up to 2 years old, and some up to 5 years old. Verify score acceptance windows directly with each medical school. Candidates may retake the MCAT within lifetime attempt limits to obtain higher or more recent scores.

retake_policy.notes: Up to 3 MCAT attempts per testing year (Jan 1–Dec 31). Up to 4 attempts in any 2 consecutive testing years. Maximum 7 lifetime attempts. All non-voided scores are reported to schools (no score selection). Score release is approximately 30-35 days after the test date. Scores from voided exams or no-shows are not reported.

domains.name: Chemical and Physical Foundations of Biological Systems (CPBS): general chemistry, physics, biochemistry, organic chemistry
Critical Analysis and Reasoning Skills (CARS): reading comprehension, inference, argument evaluation
Biological and Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems (BBFL): biology, biochemistry, organic chemistry, general chemistry
Psychological, Social, and Biological Foundations of Behavior (PSBF): psychology, sociology, biology

official_study_resources.title: AAMC Official MCAT Full-Length Practice Exam 1 (Free)
official_study_resources.notes: One free full-length practice exam available to all registered users. Identical format and question types to the real exam.

official_study_resources.title: AAMC Official MCAT Full-Length Practice Exams 2-6
official_study_resources.notes: $35 USD each; full-length scored practice exams with detailed score reports.

official_study_resources.title: AAMC Official MCAT Prep Bundle (All Products)
official_study_resources.notes: Bundle of all official AAMC prep products including full-length practice tests, section banks, question packs, and flashcards. Discounted vs. purchasing individually.

official_study_resources.title: Khan Academy MCAT Collection
official_study_resources.notes: Free, comprehensive content review created in partnership with AAMC. Covers all four MCAT sections with videos, articles, and practice questions.
